message: Unused file is not reachable from any entry point, so it adds maintenance surface without shipping any code.
help: Delete the file if it is truly unreachable, or import it from an entry point.

title: .map().filter(Boolean) loops twice
message: This loops over your list twice because .map().filter(Boolean) makes two passes, so use .flatMap() to change & drop items in one pass
help: Use `.flatMap(item => condition ? [value] : [])` to change and drop items in one pass, instead of building a throwaway array in between

message: Unused file is not reachable from any entry point, so it adds maintenance surface without shipping any code.
help: Delete the file if it is truly unreachable, or import it from an entry point.

title: Redundant manual memoization
message: This `useCallback` is dead weight, since React Compiler already caches every function here. Delete it.
help: Delete the `useMemo` / `useCallback` / `memo` call and use the plain value or component. React Compiler caches it for you.

title: useEffect setState flashes on mount
message: This flashes for your users because useEffect(setState, []) runs after the first paint, so use useSyncExternalStore, or add suppressHydrationWarning
help: Use `useSyncExternalStore(subscribe, getSnapshot, getServerSnapshot)` or add `suppressHydrationWarning` to the element

title: State initialized from a mount effect
message: Your users see an extra render with empty "lastMethod" because a useEffect sets its starting value.
help: Pass the initial value directly to useState() instead of setting it from a mount-only useEffect. For SSR hydration, prefer useSyncExternalStore().

title: React Compiler can't optimize this
message: This component misses React Compiler's automatic memoization & re-renders more than it should: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders. Rewrite the flagged code so the compiler can optimize it.
help:
Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

title: Heavy library loaded eagerly
message: "recharts" ships extra code to your users up front & slows page load. Load it on demand with React.lazy() or next/dynamic.
help: Load it only when needed: `const Component = dynamic(() => import('library'), { ssr: false })` from next/dynamic, or React.lazy().

title: Full Framer Motion import
message: Importing "motion" ships about 30 kb of extra code and slows page load. Use "m" with LazyMotion instead.
help: Use `import { LazyMotion, m } from "framer-motion"` with `domAnimation` features. Saves about 30kb.

title: State only used in handlers
message: Each update to "isInView" redraws your component for nothing because this useState is set but never shown on screen.
help: Use useRef instead of useState when the value is only set and never shown on screen. `ref.current = ...` updates it without redrawing the component.

title: Effect re-subscribes on a changing callback
message: Your effect re-subscribes whenever "updateSquares" changes, even though it's only used inside `requestAnimationFrame`.
help: Wrap the callback with `useEffectEvent(callback)` (React 19+) and call it inside the sub-handler. An Effect Event always sees the latest props and state but isn't a dependency, so the effect won't re-subscribe every time the parent redraws. See https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffectEvent

title: Effect re-subscribes on a changing callback
message: Your effect re-subscribes whenever "drawGrid" changes, even though it's only used inside `requestAnimationFrame`.
help: Wrap the callback with `useEffectEvent(callback)` (React 19+) and call it inside the sub-handler. An Effect Event always sees the latest props and state but isn't a dependency, so the effect won't re-subscribe every time the parent redraws. See https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffectEvent

title: Large component is hard to read and change
message: Component "RetroGrid" is 317 lines long, which is hard to read & change. Split it into a few smaller components.
help: Pull each section into its own component so the parent is easier to read, test, and change.

title: State initialized from a mount effect
message: Your users see an extra render with empty "isWebGlReady" because a useEffect sets its starting value.
help: Pass the initial value directly to useState() instead of setting it from a mount-only useEffect. For SSR hydration, prefer useSyncExternalStore().

title: Redundant manual memoization
message: This `memo()` is dead weight, since React Compiler already caches the component's output. Delete it.
help: Delete the `useMemo` / `useCallback` / `memo` call and use the plain value or component. React Compiler caches it for you.

title: next/image fill image is missing sizes
message: next/image uses fill without sizes, so your users download the largest image.
help: Add `sizes` matching your layout so `next/image` does not assume the largest candidate and make users download oversized images.

title: Control missing accessible label
message: Blind users can't tell what this control does because screen readers find no label, so add visible text, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`.
help: Give every interactive control a label screen readers can read.

title: Prop derived into useState
message: Your users see a stale value when prop "profile" changes because useState copies it once.
help: Compute the value inline so prop changes do not leave `useState` holding a stale copy.

title: Role used instead of HTML tag
message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<input>` than `role="slider"`, so use `<input>` instead.
help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

title: Overly precise SVG path values
message: Your users download extra bytes for SVG d precision they can't see, so round it to 1 or 2 decimals.
help: Round path, points, and transform decimals to 1 or 2 digits. The extra precision adds bytes with no visible difference.

title: React 19 API migration can break callers
message: forwardRef is dead weight in React 19, since ref is a normal prop now, so drop it & pass ref straight through.
help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

title: Label missing associated control
message: Screen reader users can't tell which input this label names because it's tied to none, so add `htmlFor` or wrap the input inside it.
help: Tie every label to a control with `htmlFor`, or by nesting the input.

title: React 19 API migration can break callers
message: useContext is replaced by `use()` in React 19, which reads context inside ifs & loops too, so switch to `import { use } from 'react'`.
help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

title: Derived value copied into state
message: Storing "internalValue" in state when you can derive it from other values costs an extra render.
help: Work out the value while rendering (or with useMemo if it's expensive) instead of copying it into useState through a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#updating-state-based-on-props-or-state

title: Zod 3 schema API breaks in Zod 4
message: This Zod 3 schema API changed in Zod 4, so this schema can fail after the upgrade.
help: Switch to the Zod 4 versions: top-level factories like `z.enum()`, object helpers like `z.strictObject()`, the new `z.function({ input, output })` form, and explicit key/value schemas for `z.record()`.

message: next@16.1.1 bundles a React Server Components runtime affected by a high-severity denial-of-service vulnerability (CVE-2026-23870) patched in Next.js 16.2.6
help: Upgrade Next.js to 16.2.6 (or newer). Next.js bundles its own React Server Components runtime, so bumping Next.js ships the fix. Run `npm install next@16.2.6`. See https://vercel.com/changelog/next-js-may-2026-security-release

message: pnpm-workspace.yaml is missing `trustPolicy` — without `no-downgrade`, pnpm silently accepts packages whose trust signals (provenance, signatures) weaken between updates
help: Add `trustPolicy: no-downgrade` to pnpm-workspace.yaml

title: Format method on z.string()
message: This `z.string().<format>()` check is deprecated in Zod 4, so it can break during the upgrade.
help: Use the Zod 4 top-level format checks like `z.email()`, `z.uuid()`, or `z.ipv4()` instead of `z.string().<format>()`.

title: Sequential independent awaits
message: This await doesn't use the previous result, so your users wait twice as long for nothing.
help: These two awaits don't depend on each other. Wrap them in `Promise.all([...])` so they run at the same time.
